_comment: Keel managed policies for Brave/Chromium on Linux. Install to /etc/brave/policies/managed/keel-managed-policy.json. These are enforced and cannot be overridden by the end user. Documentation: https://chromeenterprise.google/policies/ and https://github.com/brave/brave-browser

_keel_notes.rewards_wallet_vpn_leo_talk: Disabled via Brave policy keys. These prevent feature surfaces from appearing in the UI.
_keel_notes.safe_browsing: Kept ON. Extended reporting + deep scanning OFF for privacy. Standard protection level (1) avoids sending hashes for unrequested checks.
_keel_notes.https_only: Force-enabled at policy level so the user cannot accidentally browse plaintext.
_keel_notes.metrics: All Chromium telemetry/usage pings disabled. Brave-specific P3A/stats/ads/perf telemetry disabled via master_preferences (see docs/no-analytics.md).
_keel_notes.ChromeVariations_1: Value 1 = 'allow critical fixes only' — the variations service can still ship urgent security mitigations but not feature experiments. 0 = full block; we deliberately keep critical fixes.
_keel_notes.search: Default kept as Brave Search (privacy-preserving, configurable by user).
_keel_notes.extensions: Allowed but not pre-installed. ExtensionAllowedTypes restricts to extensions/themes only.
_keel_notes.component_updates: Kept ON. These deliver security fixes (cert revocation, Subresource Filter, etc.).
